S1.name: Foundation
S1.goal: Implement log buffer (three-term mmap) and IPC ring buffers with ≥ 90% test coverage

S2.name: QUIC Transport
S2.goal: quic-go adapter, Multi-QUIC pool, LowestRTT arbitrator, TLS 1.3 enforced

S3.name: Driver Core
S3.goal: Conductor, Sender, Receiver agents running as goroutines; hsd binary boots and shuts down cleanly

S4.name: Path Intelligence
S4.goal: Path Manager sends PING/PONG probes; per-connection RTT/loss tracked atomically

S5.name: Pool Intelligence
S5.goal: Adaptive Pool Learner right-sizes pool; Pool Manager manages connection lifecycle and cert rotation

S6.name: Client Library
S6.goal: Public Client/Publication/Subscription API; channel URI parser; end-to-end examples run in CI

S7.name: Congestion Control
S7.goal: CUBIC, BBR, BBRv3 implemented; DRL controller loads ONNX model and runs inference; BBRv3 fallback

S8.name: Observability
S8.goal: cnc.dat counters; structured slog events; OTel traces/metrics; hyperspace-stat CLI working

S9.name: AWS + Identity
S9.goal: Cloud Map discovery, SSM config, Secrets Manager certs, SPIFFE/SPIRE SVID fetch — all integration-tested via Localstack

S10.name: CI/CD, README, Examples, Benchmarks
S10.goal: CI pipeline green on all gates; README complete; benchmark scaffolding in cmd/hyperspace-probe

F-001.name: Log Buffer
F-001.description: Three-term mmap'd ring buffer: atomic appender, reader, term management, frame header
F-001.notes: Session 2026-04-17: project bootstrap; code not yet written

F-002.name: Ring Buffers and IPC
F-002.description: SPSC and MPSC ring buffers; broadcast transmitter/receiver; mmap utilities
F-002.notes: Session 2026-04-17: project bootstrap; code not yet written

F-003.name: QUIC Transport Adapter
F-003.description: quic-go wrapper implementing Hyperspace Connection interface; TLS 1.3 config

F-004.name: Multi-QUIC Connection Pool
F-004.description: N concurrent QUIC connections per peer; pool data structure

F-005.name: Connection Arbitrator
F-005.description: Strategy-based connection selection: LowestRTT, LeastOutstanding, Hybrid, Sticky, Random

F-006.name: Path Manager and Latency Probes
F-006.description: PING/PONG probe frames; per-connection RTT/loss/throughput tracking

F-007.name: Adaptive Pool Learner
F-007.description: Latency-loss correlation model; automatic pool size adjustment recommendations

F-008.name: Driver Agents (Conductor, Sender, Receiver)
F-008.description: Three cooperating goroutines: control plane, outbound data plane, inbound data plane; hsd binary

F-009.name: Pool Manager Agent
F-009.description: QUIC connection lifecycle; certificate rotation via SPIRE; integrates Adaptive Pool Learner

F-010.name: Client Library
F-010.description: Public Go API: Client, Publication, Subscription, Image; channel URI parser; examples

F-011.name: Congestion Control
F-011.description: CUBIC, BBR, BBRv3; DRL controller via ONNX Runtime (CGO); BBRv3 fallback
F-011.notes: Blocked on S2; CGO requires Architecture Evaluator awareness

F-012.name: Observability
F-012.description: Atomic named counters (cnc.dat); structured events; OTel traces/metrics; hyperspace-stat CLI

F-013.name: AWS Integration
F-013.description: Cloud Map discovery; SSM configuration; Secrets Manager certificate fetch; Localstack CI tests
F-013.notes: Blocked on S6; requires Localstack in CI

F-014.name: SPIFFE/SPIRE Identity
F-014.description: Workload identity via SPIRE workload API; X.509 SVID fetch and rotation
F-014.notes: Blocked on S6; Security Evaluator PASS required

status_legend.blocked: Cannot proceed — see notes for blocker

tech_debt.description: Q-001 (0-RTT replay risk) must be resolved before F-003 is marked passing
Q-002 (max message size / fragmentation limit) must be defined before F-001 appender is finalised
